title: English Podcast | Food and Drink
youtubeTitle: Easy English Chat About Your Favorite Food
youtubeDescription:
A slow, easy English conversation about food and drink — favorite meals, eating out, and cooking at home. Perfect for A2 beginners who want to build everyday vocabulary and sound more natural in real American and Australian conversations.

🎧 Turn on captions (CC) and just listen.
👉 Subscribe for a new easy English conversation every week!

#LearnEnglish #AmericanEnglish #ESL #A2English #FoodEnglish

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Hi everyone, and welcome back to the show! I'm Emma.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: And I'm Mike. Today we are talking about food and drink.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I love this topic! So Mike, what is your favorite food?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: My favorite food is pizza. I can eat it every day.
turns.enTts: [chuckles] My favorite food is pizza. I can eat it every day.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Really? Every day? That is a lot of pizza!

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I know! What about you, Emma? What food do you like?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I really like rice with chicken and vegetables.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That sounds healthy. Do you eat it often?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, I eat it for dinner two or three times a week.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Nice. So let's talk about meals. What do you eat for breakfast?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: For breakfast, I usually have bread, eggs, and a cup of coffee.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Coffee in the morning is the best. I need it too!

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Me too. And what do you have for breakfast, Mike?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I often eat cereal with milk. It is quick and easy.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That is a good idea for busy mornings.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes. Now, what do you eat for lunch?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: For lunch, I like a sandwich or some soup.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Do you eat lunch at home or at work?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I usually eat at work. I bring my lunch in a box.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is smart. I sometimes forget my lunch at home!
turns.enTts: [laughs] That is smart. I sometimes forget my lunch at home!

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Oh no! So what do you do then?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I buy some noodles or a small salad near my office.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Okay. And what about dinner? What do you eat at night?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: For dinner, I like fish, rice, and some fruit after.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That sounds really good. Do you drink water with dinner?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, I drink water. But I love orange juice too.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Orange juice is my favorite drink! It is fresh and sweet.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Do you make it at home or buy it?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I make it at home. I use three or four oranges.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Wow, homemade juice is the best. I want to try that!

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: So we talked about our favorite meals. Now let's talk about eating out. Do you like restaurants, Mike?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, I do! I go to a restaurant every weekend. It is fun.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Nice. What food do you like to order?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I love pizza and pasta. Italian food is my favorite. What about you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I like Japanese food. I often order sushi and rice.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Sushi is great! Do you eat with chopsticks?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, I do. It is not easy, but I like it.
